name: Ecosystem replacement bootstrap pass for June 3, 2026

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Ecosystem Replacement, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

summary: The staged baseline assigns low-confidence human_biomarker_signal. Direct aging-relevant support is animal-led: young microbiota extended killifish lifespan, wild-type donor FMT improved healthspan and lifespan in progeroid mice, and aged-mouse FMT studies report improved frailty, grip strength, body composition, cognition, gut barrier, SCFAs, inflammatory, metabolomic, and transcriptomic readouts. Human evidence supports only a bounded biomarker and implementation case: lean-donor FMT improved insulin sensitivity in a small metabolic-syndrome trial, older-adult CDI case review supports disease-context feasibility and safety, and metagenomic analyses show donor strain engraftment is measurable but variable. NCT05598112 and ARMOR/NCT06649981 are direct older-adult aging trials but had no posted efficacy results as of June 3, 2026.
notes: The top ready bootstrap queue item was selected from research/backlog/track-priority.v1.json and research/state/coverage-status.v1.json.
The public question was scoped to one dysbiosis track: ecosystem-replacement.

The staged outlook uses human_biomarker_signal because lean-donor FMT has human metabolic biomarker evidence and FMT engraftment is measurable in human cohorts, but confidence remains low because direct aging benefit is not shown in humans.

The direct aging-benefit evidence remains animal-led, with fish lifespan, progeroid mouse lifespan and healthspan, and aged-mouse frailty, cognition, barrier, inflammatory, SCFA, metabolic, and transcriptomic readouts.
Older-adult CDI evidence was treated as safety and disease-context feasibility, not aging efficacy.
NCT05598112 and ARMOR/NCT06649981 were verified through ClinicalTrials.gov API on June 3, 2026 and staged as no-results trial-watch context.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity and interpretation/forecast evidence reviews before publication.

During interpretation review, decide whether the human metabolic and engraftment evidence is sufficient for human_biomarker_signal or whether the public stage should remain animal_signal until direct older-adult aging trial results post.
Before publication, recheck NCT05598112 and NCT06649981 for status or posted results.

If approved and published, inspect the dysbiosis hallmark page and ecosystem replacement track page for taxonomy overlap with microbiome composition, live biotherapeutics, and metabolite restoration tracks.
